financial.note: Holds 45% stake; RATCH acts as the primary investment arm for EGAT's IPP projects. Financial health is deeply linked to EGAT's credit rating and the Thai sovereign outlook.

financial.entity: Siam Commercial Bank (SCB.BK) / Krung Thai Bank (KTB.BK)
financial.note: Major financiers for regional M&A and infrastructure projects; rising interest rates increase the servicing cost on their ~฿100bn+ debt load.

supply_chain.note: Revenue stability is secured via 20-25 year Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) with EGAT, though 2026 sees some impact from the expiry of the Ratchaburi Thermal Units.
Primary fuel supplier for thermal and combined-cycle plants; fuel costs are generally passed through, but PTT price volatility can impact working capital.
NEXIF RATCH Energy is the core vehicle for expansion into renewable energy in Vietnam and the Philippines, targeting 30-40% renewable capacity by 2030.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Acquired in 2024; provides significant dividend income flow (est. ฿4bn+ annually in 2026) to RATCH's consolidated EBITDA.
Completed investment restructuring in early 2026 to optimize the 1,400 MW plant's returns.
Diversification into healthcare infrastructure to create a secondary 'steady-income' stream.

competitors.note: Main rival for large-scale IPP and renewable projects; Gulf's rapid scale-up in gas and LNG-to-power pressures RATCH's market position.
Direct peer and fellow EGAT affiliate; both compete for regional M&A and international renewable energy projects.
Rival in the SPP and regional renewable segments, particularly in Vietnam and the Philippines.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The Lincoln Gap 3 Wind Farm (operational early 2026) makes RATCH's earnings increasingly sensitive to Australian environmental and energy market prices.
As a global holding company (Paiton Energy in Indonesia, Australian assets), a weaker THB boosts translated dividend and net income.
Directly impacts the cost of the ฿40bn+ 5-year investment plan and influences the yield spread for dividend investors.
